rulings.Case Title: NICOLAS GONZALEZ VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Recission Cause Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 23, 2018, Line 2. PLAINTIFF NICOLAS GONZALEZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Recission Cause Of Action. DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden on all issues for summary adjudication listed in the Notice of Motion. Rescission is available to a party who has no knowledge of dual representation. Vice v. Thacker (1947) 30 Cal.2d 84. Plaintiff provides no evidence that he had no knowledge of Mr. West's dual representation (See Gonzalez Declaration paragraph 8) and fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a written disclosure in English accompanied by oral explanation of the terms in Spanish amounts to "no knowledge" warranting rescission as a matter of law. Plaintiff further provides no evidence that Castillo was buyer's agent, i.e. that Castillo was a dual agent for Gonzalez and buyer. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that undisclosed alleged co-agency of West and Castillo for Gonzalez is basis for rescission. As to the 23rd affirmative defense, while it is mentioned in the Notice of Motion and briefly addressed in the Points and Authorities without any reference to the Separate Statement, it is not listed in the Separate Statement. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLAS GONZALEZ VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 23, 2018, Line 2. DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC'S DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled, 10 days to answer. Fifth Amended Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty against the moving defendant. The Court notes Defendant's failure to comply with CRC 2.108. In the future non-compliant and oversized briefs will be stricken. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER VARGAS VS. BARBARA FEDUN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Overrule Defendants Objections As To The Deposition Of Third Party Witness Alexene Shore
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 23, 2018, Line 3. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER VARGAS' Motion To Overrule Defendants Objections As To The Deposition Of Third Party Witness Alexene Shore. Plaintiff's request to overrule Defendant's warning regarding reservation of rights as to deposition questions potentially subject to confidentially agreement is DENIED. Plaintiff cites not authority for the proposition that the Court may overrule party's contractual rights, especially as here, such ruling is sought prospectively before objection is made to any specific question. Hearing Required to address whether deposition shall proceed after discovery cut-off date to address whether third party explicitly refused to appear or Plaintiff cancelled the deposition without such refusal. Any evidence of explicit refusal to appear to be submitted no later than 9:00 a.m. on February 23, 2018. =(501/REQ)
